text: Glacier National
zh: 冰川国家公园

text: Spanning the Continental Divide and expanding from Northwest Montana
zh: 跨越大陆分水岭，从蒙大拿州西北部延伸

text: into the Canadian provinces of Alberta and British Columbia is Glacier National Park.
zh: 一直到加拿大的艾伯塔省和不列颠哥伦比亚省，这就是冰川国家公园。

text: Here, on the border of Montana, sprawls one million acres of National Park, one of the world's great mountain landscapes.
zh: 这里，在蒙大拿州边境，铺展着一百万英亩的国家公园土地，是世界上最壮丽的山地景观之一。

text: Glacier National Park is more than a preserve.
zh: 冰川国家公园不仅仅是一片保护区。

text: It is an outreach to Waterton Lakes National Park in Canada,
zh: 它是通向加拿大沃特顿湖国家公园的一次伸展，

text: an alpine back fence over which American and Canada clasp each other's hands and say, Peace, neighbor.
zh: 是一道高山边界，美国和加拿大在此握手言和，说：和平，邻邦。

text: And so these two parks, Canada's and America's,
zh: 于是这两座公园，加拿大的和美国的，

text: form the world's first international peace park, Waterton Glacier,
zh: 共同组成了世界上第一座国际和平公园——沃特顿冰川国际和平公园，

text: memorializing the longest unguarded border on Earth. This is the crown of the continent.
zh: 纪念着地球上最长的不设防边界。这里是大陆之冠。

text: Glacier National Park itself is a garden of ice sculpture, as though chiseled by some titan, some mythic Michelangelo.
zh: 冰川国家公园本身就是一座冰雕花园，仿佛出自巨人之手，出自某位神话中的米开朗基罗。

text: Hiking among myriad glaciers, nearly half a hundred of them.
zh: 在近五十座冰川之间徒步穿行。

text: 10 ,000 years ago, these mountains were overwhelmed by onslaughts of ice.
zh: 一万年前，这些山脉曾被冰川的猛烈冲击所淹没。

text: in juggernaut descents down mountain sides. Gouging.
zh: 冰川如巨兽般沿山坡倾泻而下，切割大地。

text: Scooping out valleys. When the ice melts, land emerges.
zh: 挖出一道道山谷。冰雪消融后，陆地显现，

text: making lakes of meltwater.
zh: 融水汇成湖泊。

text: The harsh landscape is softened by a down of shy grass.
zh: 荒凉的地貌渐渐被柔嫩的青草覆盖，变得柔和。

text: Forests grow.
zh: 森林生长起来。

text: Flowers bloom. And wildlife proliferates.
zh: 鲜花绽放，野生动物繁衍生息。

text: For millennia, it thrives untouched. until
zh: 数千年间，这片土地未经触碰，蓬勃生长，直到

text: a tribe of Native Americans belonging to the powerful Blackfeet Nation make this wilderness their home.
zh: 一支属于强大黑脚族的印第安部落，把这片荒野当作了家园。

text: To them, this is a sacred place,
zh: 对他们来说，这里是一片圣地，

text: with good hunting for meat and hides, and remedial plants for medicine.
zh: 有充足的猎物可获取肉食和皮毛，也有可入药的草木。

text: In the language of the Blackfeet, this is the backbone of the world.
zh: 在黑脚族的语言中，这里被称为“世界的脊梁”。

text: After centuries of silence, broken only by birdsong, the moanings of a train whistle,
zh: 经过数百年只有鸟鸣打破的寂静之后，火车汽笛的呜咽声响起，

text: Billows of smoke. Steam power.
zh: 滚滚浓烟。蒸汽动力。

text: With multiple blasts through the Rocky Mountains, the Great Northern Line completed America's first continental railroad.
zh: 经过多次穿越落基山脉的爆破工程，大北方铁路线完成了美国第一条横贯大陆的铁路。

text: Passengers were encouraged to deboard at the newly created Glacier National Park.
zh: 乘客们被鼓励在新建成的冰川国家公园下车。

text: Today Glacier is one of the few national parks with an Amtrak train stop inside the gates. Door to door travel.
zh: 如今，冰川国家公园是少数几个在园区内设有美铁火车站的国家公园之一，实现了门到门的旅行。

text: Swiss -style chalets and lodges, some over a century old, offer folksy out -west hospitality.
zh: 瑞士风格的小木屋和旅舍，有些已有上百年历史，提供质朴的西部式待客之道。

text: The Glacier Park Lodge is architecturally harmonious with its natural surroundings. buildings.
zh: 冰川公园旅舍在建筑风格上与周围的自然环境和谐相融。

text: Inside, supporting its high roof, are pillars of Douglas firs from Oregon, 40 feet high.
zh: 内部支撑高高屋顶的，是来自俄勒冈州的花旗松柱子，高达40英尺。

text: Native Blackfeet call this hostelry Big Tree Lodge.
zh: 当地黑脚族人称这座旅舍为“大树旅舍”。

text: Lake McDonald Lodge is built of rough -hewn cedar.
zh: 麦克唐纳湖旅舍由粗砍的雪松木建成。

text: Inside are reminders of its hunting lodge origins.
zh: 内部的陈设让人想起它最初作为狩猎小屋的历史。

text: only way to reach some of these chalets is by foot or horseback.
zh: 有些小木屋唯一的到达方式是徒步或骑马。

text: But since 1933, autos have been the choice means of getting around the park.
zh: 但自1933年起，汽车成了游览公园的首选交通方式，

text: via a highway named Going to the Sun Road, an engineering marvel.
zh: 沿着一条名为“阳光之路”的公路而行，这是一项工程奇迹。

text: This is one of the most scenic roadways in the world,
zh: 这是世界上风景最壮丽的公路之一，

text: winding up and over the Continental Divide at Logan Pass.
zh: 蜿蜒而上，穿越洛根隘口，翻越大陆分水岭。

text: Short trips from hotel to hotel can be made on picturesque 1930s buses,
zh: 从一家旅舍到另一家的短途旅程，可以乘坐颇具风情的1930年代老式巴士完成，

text: completely restored, called Red Jammers. summers.
zh: 这些巴士经过全面修复，被称为“红色颠簸车”，专门在夏季运营。

text: Many areas in the park have boardwalks to protect the fragile flora.
zh: 公园内许多区域都铺设了栈道，以保护脆弱的植被。

text: For the seasoned outdoorsman, a monumental hike on the park's myriad trails may be the choice.
zh: 对经验丰富的户外爱好者来说，在公园众多小径上进行一次盛大的徒步或许是最佳选择。

text: These trails span most of the park, north to south,
zh: 这些步道纵贯公园南北，

text: from the banks of glacial lakes to alpine heights.
zh: 从冰川湖畔一直延伸到高山之巅。

text: The U .S. and Canada Glacier Waterton Parks.
zh: 美国与加拿大的冰川沃特顿公园，

text: These are a unique monument to world peace.
zh: 是世界和平的独特象征。

text: The hope for peace in most every human heart.
zh: 和平的希望，存在于几乎每一颗人类的心中。
